nchfp_dry.name: National Center for Home Food Preservation

items: American Business: The Industrial Revolution helped launch the modern industrial era in the United States.
American Heritage: The Declaration of Independence says, “We hold these truths to be self-evident.”
American Labor: Organized labor helped shape major workplace protections and standards in the United States.
Animal Science: Cattle pregnancies average about 280 days; pigs average about 114 days.
Animation: Rapidly displayed images create the illusion of movement.
Archaeology: Comparing artifacts with objects from dated sites can help estimate a site’s relative age.
Archery: Modern arrow shafts are commonly made from wood, aluminum, carbon, or fiberglass.
Architecture: Reusing an existing building can conserve materials and reduce demolition waste.
Art: Tempera uses a water-soluble binder and is known for strong, durable color.
Astronomy: A star’s color is a major clue to its surface temperature.
Aviation: Pilots account for wind, magnetic variation, and compass deviation when planning headings.
Backpacking: Drying food removes water, making many trail meals lighter and easier to store.
Basketry: Rattan comes from climbing palms whose flexible stems can be processed into cane and reed.
Bird Study: Beaks, feet, wings, and feathers are adapted to how each bird lives and feeds.
Bugling: Like other brass instruments, a bugle begins with the player’s vibrating lips.
Camping: A map, compass, or GPS is useful only when you know how to establish and track your position.
Chemistry: Safety Data Sheets explain chemical hazards, handling, storage, and emergency precautions.
Citizenship in the Nation: Thomas Paine wrote that America’s cause was “the cause of all mankind.”
Citizenship in Society: An upstander takes safe, constructive action instead of ignoring bullying.
Coin Collecting: “Proof” describes a special coin-making process, not a condition grade.
Cycling: A properly fitted bicycle helmet reduces the risk of serious head injury.
Dentistry: A knocked-out permanent tooth may be saved if handled by the crown and treated quickly.
Digital Technology: A browser cookie stores small pieces of website data, such as session or preference information.
Disabilities Awareness: Captioned phones, relay services, and videophones can make communication more accessible.
Dog Care: Vaccination plans depend on a dog’s age, health, lifestyle, and disease exposure risk.
